message: Jake
Yuriko
Tim
At one point, I was wondering what would happen, but it seems the detectives don't care about me at all.
What's with that disappointed tone?
Tim
Not really.
It's not that, but it just made me think, what the hell were they even here for?
Sure enough, judging by that behavior, they might suspect the same as in Eva's case this time too.
Tim
I'm heading home. Sorry for dragging this nonsense into the morning.
Nah, don't worry about it. Come hang out again next time.
Tim
Yeah, thanks. See ya.
And then there was no one left.
That's how it feels.
It was a lively and tiring morning.
Ah, Boss! Welcome back.
I'm home.
Yeah, I'm tired.

It's the usual dimly lit office with the evening sunlight streaming in. The flickering fluorescent light hurts my eyes.
The smell of cigarettes and mold clings to the bodies of the detectives, still lingering in the office. It seems I'll never forget it.
It's just me and
no one else. This feels more calming after all.
is looking at me, completely unfazed as if nothing had happened. Doesn't this kid have any fears?
A small build typical of the Japanese, with slightly brown hair and jet-black eyes. Normally, I wouldn't have to pay an assistant.
But I still have a lingering feeling about the time Meryl and Cindy faced each other,
but it seems they've already forgotten.
Cindy
is dead, huh? I didn't really feel it until just now, but now I realize.
The situation was hard to understand, but another body part was delivered.
Just like with Ginny.
So the culprit is?
Once I started getting work, unpleasant things began happening, didn’t they? It feels like I'm cursed.
I don't like that, don’t talk like that.
Because just like this,
everything was peaceful and serene until suddenly, we fell into this situation?
New York really is scary. I think living alone here would definitely be frightening.
If it's anything, you can quit.
Huh?
That is
still
Yes, I'll stay here at least until this incident is resolved. Please let me stay.
Please, Boss. Until this incident is over.
What about Cindy with Meryl?
I've told her everything. But,
I feel pathetic, really. If I had spoken up sooner, there might have been a different future.
It's too late to blame yourself now. Right now, we can only think about how to support Meryl moving forward.
Meryl isn’t going to commit suicide, right?
Isn’t it okay not to tell Meryl about Cindy?
No, it's my fault for having hidden it all this time, so I have to tell her everything myself.
That's true.
Meryl will be shocked to learn that Cindy was her daughter, and that she died all at once.
That’s so sad.
According to Günter, Kristy's symptoms have worsened again.
What? Again? Kristy seems unstable. Is she going to be okay?
I hate to say this, but I'm worried about Günter. I wonder if this psychiatrist is really reliable.
Oh come on.
That’s a harsh way to put it. I think it’s too much.
I thought about it, but
After all, Kristy needs Guy, no matter what kind of guy he is.
That makes Guy sound like a terrible man.
He's bad enough, just disappearing somewhere and leaving his fiancée behind.
It's really sad for Kristy.
I don’t know what to say. That girl has been here, right?
Yeah, it was when she mentioned thanking us at FLIX.
Yes.
At that time, I recognized how cute she was. She was so charming. It felt like she was glowing.
Why does such a good girl have to be killed?
It feels very unreasonable.
Maybe she was just too cute and was wished for by the gods.
Oh, come on.
If that's the case, I don’t want that kind of god.
Who on earth would do this to Cindy?
Gloria might have said this before, but she loved Cindy, right?
Of course she must have loved her.
Not just in that sense, there must have been a physical relationship too.
Saying something like that...
I really don’t care for that kind of gossip.
In any case, Gloria who has lost Cindy must be devastated.
From now on, will she raise a second Cindy?
As long as she’s doing her current job, she’ll have to.
The dead are forgotten, huh? I don’t want that.
You might not forget, but those left behind have to keep living.
That might be true, but...
But I absolutely won’t forget.
Will Gloria forget about Cindy?
Tim seemed fine, didn’t he? Maybe he’s surprisingly not that shocked?
That’s not true. It’s probably just that it doesn’t feel real yet. I think he’ll drown in deep sorrow from now on.
Y-Yeah, you’re right.
Even if he seems normal, it doesn’t mean he's heartless.
I hope Tim can recover quickly.
You went to the shop that Tony goes to, right?
Yeah, I did.
So, what was it like? Did anyone talk to you?
The girl named Anju, Tony's girlfriend, spoke to me. It was lucky to avoid the trouble of searching.
S-So what happened between you two?
What happened between the two of them?
For now, I went along with it until morning. I got the feeling that Tonny is going through a lot, but he seems to be a guy who's unrelated to killing or kidnapping.
Huh?
Y-you're really taking what Tonny says at face value? That's playing right into his hands!
That kid doesn’t seem like the type to lie, and honestly, I think he answered truthfully.
Wait, does 'went along until morning' actually mean...!? Boss!
Boss!
I believe in you, Boss.
So about the Serial Murder Case, it was rumored that it had been occurring all over before coming to New York, right?
Yeah, I think that's what was said.
Then why is it happening one after another in New York? Assuming, of course, that all the recent ones are actually that.
If Ginny's disappearance and the cases of Eva and Cindy are all the work of the serial killer,
the culprit might have some reason to stay in New York and is forced to commit crimes in this city.
Huh?
N-no way!
If that’s the case, it means this killer has no sense of aesthetic when it comes to their crimes.
Aesthetic of crime!? I wonder if this killer even has that.
Absolutely, they do! There must be a reason behind chopping up bodies and taking them home.
That's true, but...
No matter how you look at it, I just can’t accept it. It's strange to commit multiple crimes in New York.
Is it possible that the killer really likes this city?
Meryl probably doesn't have the energy to keep working anymore. Though, knowing her, she doesn’t have to worry about starving.
Since the director is there too, Meryl will surely be able to bounce back.
Actually, your daughter has been killed.
I have to say it, don’t I? I can’t run away from this.
It's weighing heavily on me.
There's no helping it, it's my responsibility.
Maybe what Günter feared has become reality, Kristy is a murderer.
I wonder if there’s any hope for Kristy to recover. Even someone inexperienced like me can see she's in serious condition.
I can only pray she manages to pull through. It’s frustrating not being able to do anything.
To have dismembered the body and sent a part back. It's a desecration of the dead. I want to bury Cindy quickly.
She was certainly a beautiful girl. Because of that, many children face terrible fates.
I never thought I would have to witness that.
At the very least, I need to resolve this case as quickly as possible. To honor Cindy's death, we must catch the culprit and punish them.
Seeing that figure is agonizing. If only she had been a bit tougher, she might have been able to get back on her feet.
To lose a girl as precious as a daughter or sister along with a business partner. What words can I even offer?
The news about Cindy must have reached Gloria too. I wonder what her reaction will be?
Anyway, I’ll try to meet Gloria. If she hasn’t committed suicide yet, I should be able to find her there.
I might have hurt Tim even more by asking unnecessary questions. Being a detective is a tough job.
It's true Cindy won't be coming back, but catching the culprit isn't meaningless, to prevent another Tim from being created.
Was there anything unusual around Cindy? The one closest to her was Tim; I have to ask him.
Meeting Tim again hurts my heart. If possible, I'd like to avoid it, but
that can’t be helped.
From what Anju said, I think that probably refers to the day Ginny disappeared.
That means Tony going to Anju after parting with Ginny might actually be unrelated to the case.
Sure, there's no denying how suspicious it is, but if that's just how he is.
I wonder how he'd react if I told him I met Anju.
I still don't know whether the Serial Murder Case is related to Ginny, Eva, and Cindy's incidents.
However, including the missing Ginny, the three of them, Eva and Cindy,
the series of events involving dismembered bodies in New York, could it be that the culprit just mimicked the Serial Murder?
The aesthetics of the crime, huh?
Indeed, it’s hard to believe a culprit who had been moving around suddenly stayed in New York.
I wonder if Eva and Cindy's cases are related to that series of Serial Murders?
※※※ It's a bug. ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
